---
title: "ST Multivariate Analysis"
author: "Alejandra Arjon"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
## Loading data and formating date
```{r}
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

catalog <- read.table("../data/SearchResults.txt", header = T)

# Prepare and format time
# UTC format
catalog <- catalog %>%
  mutate(
    # Combine columns into a single string "YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS.ss"
    full_time_str = paste(YYY.MM.DD, HH, mm, SS.ss, sep = " "),
    # Convert string to a date-time object
    date_time = as.POSIXct(full_time_str, format="%Y/%m/%d %H %M %OS", tz="UTC"))
```

## Creating grid
```{r}
#-121 to -114 and latitude 32 to 37
# Some points are at LAT 32, so region has to be expanded a bit
# Adding spatial bins to events 
# Convert catalog into sf object
# coordinate reference system (CRS) = 4326 (WGS84), 0.1x0.1 degrees is roughly 11km x 11km at the equator, but the width shrinks as you move toward the poles
catalog <- st_as_sf(catalog, coords = c("LON", "LAT"),crs = 4326)
#Get the region and expand it by a small margin of 0.1 degrees
bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin = -121.1, xmax = -113.9, ymin = 31.9, ymax = 37.1), crs = 4326)
# Define pixel size to 0.1 x 0.1  degrees
grid <- st_make_grid(bbox, cellsize = c(0.1, 0.1), square = TRUE) %>%
  st_sf() %>%
  mutate(grid_id = row_number())

# create a new catalog with spatial bins
events_binned <- st_join(catalog, grid)
events_binned <- events_binned[,-c(1:4, 14)]
events_binned <- st_as_sf(events_binned, coords = c("LON", "LAT"),crs = 4326)
head(events_binned)
summary(events_binned)
#earthquake threshold
threshold <- 4.5
```

### Plot of grid with events

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
#Load the California state boundary
ca_state <- subset(map_data("state"), region == "california")
ca_counties <- subset(map_data("county"), region == "california")

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = ca_counties, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), 
               fill = "lightgray", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) + 
  
  # Background: State Outline
  geom_polygon(data = ca_state, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), 
               fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) + 
  # Plot the grid squares (optional)
  geom_sf(data = grid, fill = NA, color = "lightgrey") + 
  # Plot the earthquake points
  geom_sf(data = events_binned|> 
         dplyr::arrange(MAG), aes(color = MAG, alpha = MAG)) +
  coord_sf(xlim = range(-121.1,-113.9), 
           ylim = range(31.9,37.2)) + 
  # Use a color scale that highlights intensity
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Earthquake Events Map",
       subtitle = "Colored by magnitude",
       color = "Magnitude",
       size = "Magnitude")

```


#### Y
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(Matrix) # Stores indices with data only.

# Filter by threshold and extract the date (ignoring time)
events_filtered <- events_binned %>%
  filter(MAG >= threshold) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date_time)) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() # Remove spatial data to speed up processing

# Create a sequence of all days in catalog range
all_days <- seq(min(as.Date(catalog$date_time)), max(as.Date(catalog$date_time)), by="day")

# Use table() to count events per day per grid_id
# We factorize grid_id and date to ensure every cell/day is represented
event_counts <- table(
  factor(events_filtered$date, levels = as.character(all_days)),
  factor(events_filtered$grid_id, levels = 1:nrow(grid))
)

# Convert to a matrix (1 if event with 4.5 >= MAG occurred, 0 otherwise)
Y <- (event_counts > 0)*1
#Gets rid of dimension names. Col.Names: grid id; Row Names: Date
dimnames(Y) <- NULL

```


### X1

```{r}
# Ensure we are working with a projected CRS (meters) for accurate 1km checks
# CRS(meters)EPSG:32611 is for SoCal
events_binned <- st_transform(events_binned, 32611)
threshold_events <- events_binned %>% filter(MAG >= threshold)

# Count prior events within 1km and 6 hours
counts <- sapply(1:nrow(threshold_events), function(i) {
  target <- threshold_events[i, ]
  
  # Time Filter: Find ALL events (any MAG) within 6 hours PRIOR to this target
  time_diffs <- difftime(target$date_time, events_binned$date_time, units = "hours")
  potential_indices <- which(time_diffs > 0 & time_diffs <= 6)
  
  if (length(potential_indices) == 0) return(0)
  
  # Distance Filter: Check those time-valid events for the 1km (1000m) constraint
  # This only calculates distance for the small subset of time-valid events
  dists <-  as.numeric(st_distance(target, events_binned[potential_indices, ]))
  sum(dists <= 1000)
})

# Map counts back to your filtered data frame
events_filtered$prior_count <- counts

#Create the final Matrix (X)
# We use xtabs to sum the 'prior_count' values into the Date x Grid_ID structure
event_matrix_counts <- xtabs(
  prior_count ~ factor(date, levels = as.character(all_days)) + 
                factor(grid_id, levels = 1:nrow(grid)), 
  data = events_filtered
)

X <- as.matrix(event_matrix_counts)
dimnames(X) <- NULL
```


##### ST- DBSCAN
```{r}
library(stdbscan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(maps)

# Get California counties and filter for Southern California
ca_counties <- st_as_sf(map("county", "california", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE))


# Prepare your numeric inputs
# x, y are spatial coords; t is numeric time (e.g., days or hours since start)
coords <- st_coordinates(events_binned)
time_numeric <- as.numeric(difftime(events_binned$date_time, min(events_binned$date_time), units = "hours"))
data = cbind(x = coords[,1], y = coords[,2], t = time_numeric)
# Run ST-DBSCAN
# eps_spatial: distance in degrees (0.05 ~ 5.5km)
# eps_temporal: distance in hours (e.g., 24 hours)
# min_pts: minimum events to form a cluster
st_clusters <- st_dbscan(data,
  eps_spatial = 0.1, 
  eps_temporal = 6, 
  min_pts = 5
)

# Add cluster IDs back to your data
events_binned$st_cluster <- as.factor(st_clusters$cluster)

```

```{r}
ggplot(events_binned %>% filter(st_cluster != -1 & st_cluster != 0)) + # Remove noise
  geom_sf(data = ca_counties, fill = "gray95", color = "gray80") + 
  geom_sf(aes(color = st_cluster), alpha = 0.6) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-121.1, -113.9), ylim = c(31.9, 37.1), crs = 4326) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Spatio-Temporal Earthquake Clusters ")+
  theme(legend.position = "none") # Remove all legends
```


```{r}
table(clean_data_filtered$st_cluster)
```

### Dunn index and DB index
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(clValid)
library(clusterSim)
# Filter out clusters that have fewer than 5 points and noise
clean_data_filtered <- events_binned %>%
  filter(st_cluster != 0 & st_cluster != -1) %>%
  group_by(st_cluster) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>% 
  ungroup()

# Check if we still have at least 2 clusters left to compare
if(length(unique(clean_data_filtered$st_cluster)) > 1) {
  
  numeric_data <- sf::st_coordinates(clean_data_filtered)
  cluster_ids  <- as.numeric(as.character(clean_data_filtered$st_cluster))
  dist_matrix  <- dist(numeric_data)

  # Run Dunn
  dunn_val <- dunn(dist_matrix, cluster_ids)
  print(paste("Dunn Index:", dunn_val))

  # Run Davies-Bouldin
  db_result <- index.DB(numeric_data, cluster_ids)
  print(paste("Davies-Bouldin Index:", db_result$DB))
  
} else {
  print("Not enough multi-point clusters to calculate indices.")
}
```


### Silhouette value 
```{r}
library(cluster)
scaled_matrix <- scale(cbind(coords[,1], coords[,2], time_numeric))
# Filter out noise points before calculating silhouette
# Silhouette is not defined for noise in density-based clustering
is_not_noise <- events_binned$st_cluster != "0"
clustered_data <- scaled_matrix[is_not_noise, ]
cluster_ids <- as.numeric(as.character(events_binned$st_cluster[is_not_noise]))

#  Calculate Silhouette
# Note: For millions of rows, dist() may fail due to memory limits.
# Use a subset of your data if needed.
dists <- dist(clustered_data)
sil <- silhouette(cluster_ids, dists)

```


```{r}
# Extract the summary statistics
sil_summary <- summary(sil)
avg_widths <- sil_summary$clus.avg.widths

barplot(sort(avg_widths, decreasing = TRUE), 
        main = "Average Silhouette Width per Cluster",
        xlab = "Cluster Index", ylab = "Average Width",
        col = "steelblue", border = "white")
abline(h = 0.5, col = "red", lty = 2) # Threshold for "good" clustering

```


### PCA
```{r}
# load the fast PCA package
library(irlba)

# Perform Fast PCA
# We target the top 20 components (nv = 20) instead of all possible p components.
# Center = TRUE is crucial to look at variance from the mean.
pca_fast <- irlba(X, nv = 20, center = colMeans(X))

# Analyze the Results
# 'd' contains the singular values. We square them to get eigenvalues/variance.
variance_explained <- pca_fast$d^2 / (nrow(X) - 1)
percent_variance <- variance_explained / sum(apply(X, 2, var)) * 100

# Create the Scree Plot
# This helps you see how many components are actually useful.
plot(percent_variance, type = "b", 
     main = "Scree Plot: Variance Explained by PCs",
     xlab = "Principal Component", ylab = "Variance (%)")

# Transform Data 
# Multiply your original matrix X by the rotation matrix (v) to get PC scores.
X_reduced <- X %*% pca_fast$v
```


```{r}
library(sf)
library(maps)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Get the California map and ensure it matches your grid's CRS
ca_map <- st_as_sf(map("county", "california", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE))
ca_map <- st_transform(ca_map, st_crs(grid)) # Align CRS (likely 4326)

# 2. Plot the PCA Map
ggplot() +
  # Layer 1: The Background Map
  geom_sf(data = ca_map, fill = "gray80", color = "gray25") +
  
  # Layer 2: The PCA Loadings Grid
  # grid_map is your sf grid object joined with the loadings from the previous step
  geom_sf(
  data = events_binned[events_binned$MAG >= threshold, ] |> 
         dplyr::arrange(MAG), # This puts high MAG at the bottom of the data/top of the plot
  aes(color = MAG)
) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma") +
  geom_sf(data = grid_map, aes(fill = PC1), color = NA, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Layer 3: Visual Styling
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#0571b0",  # Dark Blue (Negative Loading)
    mid = "white",     # Neutral
    high = "#ca0020", # Dark Red (Positive Loading)
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  
  # Zoom to Southern California bounding box
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-121.1, -113.9), ylim = c(31.9, 37.1), expand = FALSE) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Principal Component 1",
    fill = "PC Weight"
  )
```


```{r}
ggplot() +
  # Layer 1: The Background Map
  geom_sf(data = ca_map, fill = "gray80", color = "gray25") +
  
  # Layer 2: The PCA Loadings Grid
  # grid_map is your sf grid object joined with the loadings from the previous step
  geom_sf(data = events_binned[events_binned$MAG>=threshold,]|> 
         dplyr::arrange(MAG), aes(color = MAG)) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma") +
  geom_sf(data = grid_map, aes(fill = PC2), color = NA, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Layer 3: Visual Styling
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#0571b0",  # Dark Blue (Negative Loading)
    mid = "white",     # Neutral
    high = "#ca0020", # Dark Red (Positive Loading)
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  
  # Zoom to your specific Southern California bounding box
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-121.1, -113.9), ylim = c(31.9, 37.1), expand = FALSE) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Principal Component 2",
    fill = "PC Weight"
  )
```


#### LightGBM

```{r}
library(lightgbm)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

# Reshape Matrices to Long Format (Flattening)
# We convert the Date x Grid matrices into a single table where each row is a (Date, Grid) pair.
# This results in (n * p) rows.
data_long <- data.table(
  y = as.vector(Y), 
  prior_count = as.vector(X)
)

# n = rows (days), p = cols (grid cells)
n <- nrow(Y)
p <- ncol(Y)

data_long[, grid_id := rep(1:p, each = n)]
data_long[, day_index := rep(1:n, times = p)]

# Create Train/Test Split (Time-based split is best for forecasting)
# Let's use the first 80% of days for training, last 20% for testing
split_idx <- floor(n * 0.8)
train_rowsO <- data_long[day_index <= split_idx]
test_rowsO <- data_long[day_index > split_idx]

#  Convert to LightGBM Dataset Format
# This format is memory-efficient and required for the lightgbm function
dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(
  data = as.matrix(train_rowsO[, .(prior_count, grid_id)]), 
  label = train_rowsO$y
)

#  Set Parameters
# Since earthquakes are rare (mostly 0s), we use 'is_unbalance = TRUE' 
# or 'scale_pos_weight' to help the model find the 1s.
params <- list(
  objective = "binary",
  metric = "auc",         # Area Under Curve is better for rare events than Accuracy
  learning_rate = 0.05,
  num_leaves = 31,        # Main complexity control
  is_unbalance = TRUE,    # Crucial for seismic data where Y=1 is rare
  boost_from_average = TRUE
)

# Train the Model
modelO <- lgb.train(
  params = params,
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = 100,          # Number of boosting iterations
  valids = list(test = lgb.Dataset(
    data = as.matrix(test_rowsO[, .(prior_count, grid_id)]), 
    label = test_rowsO$y
  )),
  early_stopping_rounds = 10
)

# Predict and Evaluate
predsO <- predict(modelO, as.matrix(test_rowsO[, .(prior_count, grid_id)]))
# Resulting 'preds' is a probability between 0 and 1

```

```{r}
library(lightgbm)
library(pROC)
library(data.table)

X_pca <- X_reduced[, 1:2]

# Create the long-format data table
# y is your flattened Y matrix (n * p)
model_data_pca <- data.table(
  y = as.vector(Y),
  PC1 = rep(X_pca[, 1], each = nrow(Y) / nrow(X_pca)), # Ensure alignment
  PC2 = rep(X_pca[, 2], each = nrow(Y) / nrow(X_pca))
)

# Split into Train (80%) and Test (20%)
train_idx <- sample(1:nrow(model_data_pca), 0.8 * nrow(model_data_pca))
train_data_pca <- model_data_pca[train_idx, ]
test_data_pca <- model_data_pca[-train_idx, ]

# Create LightGBM datasets
dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(train_data_pca[, .(PC1, PC2)]), label = train_data_pca$y)

# Set parameters for binary classification
params <- list(
  objective = "binary",
  metric = "auc",
  is_unbalance = TRUE, # Handling the rare earthquake events
  learning_rate = 0.1
)

# Train
model_pca <- lgb.train(params = params, data = dtrain, nrounds = 100)

preds_pca <- predict(model_pca, as.matrix(test_data_pca[, .(PC1, PC2)]))
```


```{r}
library(PRROC)
library(data.table)
# Calculate PR Curves
pr_pca  <- pr.curve(scores.class0 = preds_pca, weights.class0 = test_data_pca$y, curve = TRUE)
pr_orig <- pr.curve(scores.class0 = predsO, weights.class0 = test_rowsO$y, curve = TRUE)

# Plot Comparison
plot(pr_pca, color = "#228be6", auc.main = FALSE, main = "Precision-Recall Comparison")
plot(pr_orig, add = TRUE, color = "#fa5252")

legend("right", 
       legend = c(paste("PCA Model (AUC:", round(pr_pca$auc.integral, 3), ")"),
                paste("Original Model (AUC:", round(pr_orig$auc.integral, 3), ")")),
       col = c("#228be6", "#fa5252"), lty = 1, lwd = 2)
```


```{r}
library(pROC)

roc_pca  <- roc(test_data_pca$y, preds_pca)
roc_orig <- roc(test_rowsO$y, predsO)

plot(roc_pca, col = "#228be6", lwd = 3, main = "ROC Curve Comparison")
lines(roc_orig, col = "#fa5252", lwd = 3)

legend("bottomright", 
       legend = c(paste("PCA (AUC:", round(auc(roc_pca), 3), ")"),
                paste("Original (AUC:", round(auc(roc_orig), 3), ")")),
       col = c("#228be6", "#fa5252"), lty = 1, lwd = 3)
```

```{r}
# X1 prediction
# High-Precision Threshold (only predict 1 if confidence is > 80%)
y_pred_high_precisionO <- ifelse(predsO > 0.80, 1, 0)

library(MLmetrics)

# Calculate Precision
precision_scoreO <- Precision(y_true = test_rowsO$y, y_pred = y_pred_high_precisionO)

# View the Confusion Matrix to see the "False Alarms" (Top Right cell)
table(Predicted = y_pred_high_precisionO, Actual = test_rowsO$y)

#X_reduced/PC prediction
y_pred_high_precision_pca <- ifelse(preds_pca > 0.80, 1, 0)
# Calculate Precision
precision_score_pca <- Precision(y_true = test_data_pca$y, y_pred = y_pred_high_precision_pca)
precision_score_pca
# View the Confusion Matrix to see the "False Alarms" (Top Right cell)
table(Predicted = y_pred_high_precision_pca, Actual = test_data_pca$y)
```
